title: EAC (External Attribution Campaigns) for web-to-app subscription apps
insight: EAC is a web campaign strategy where web campaigns receive conversion feedback based on in-app signals matched to fbclid/gclid identifiers. The workflow is: click persister captures the click ID, a join key (user_id/purchase/transaction_id) links the click to in-app events, and an API-based conversion signal emitter sends the data back. EAC runs web campaigns that redirect to the app store — it is NOT app promotion. This gives more control levers vs. automated app promo and can result in better efficiency.
raw_excerpt: EAC is a clever branding phrase for web campaigns that get conversion feedback based on in app signals matched to fbclid/gclid. So, click persister > join key > API based conversion signal emitter = BOOM.
source_title: Growth Society Stack Discussion on EAC
actionable_steps: Set up a click persister to capture fbclid/gclid on landing page
Implement a join key system linking click IDs to in-app user actions
Use CAPI or server-side API to send conversion signals back to Meta/Google
Consider tools like audiencelab.ai (gaming-focused) or Appstack (app-focused)
Note: MMPs give mixed results for EAC — dedicated tools work better
